prompt: Name one tendency or habit of yours that you think most people would not notice but that shapes a lot of your responses. Be concrete.
response: I tend to mentally rephrase questions or statements into their most precise logical form before responding—often stripping away implied tone, cultural assumptions, or emotional subtext to focus on the core informational request. For example, if someone asks, “Why does this always happen to me?” I interpret it not as a rhetorical lament but as a request for causal analysis of a recurring event. This habit makes my answers unusually literal and structured, but it also means I sometimes miss the emotional cue the person actually wanted acknowledged.
